changes.result: RISK SURFACED — KEY STRUCTURAL DIFFERENCE FROM P1-2/P1-3
changes.finding: Both P1-2 (kidney rail active) and P1-3 (cbc rail active) could emit a scored domain row using existing scoring rails. Thyroid cannot — hormonal system_weight is 0.0 and biomarkers list is empty. Cursor faces three options: (a) modify scoring_policy.yaml hormonal system to include TSH/FT3/FT4 — only permissible if all 7 conditions at prompt lines 350-364 are met; (b) implement thyroid as an unscored domain card using card evidence alone (if architecture supports it); (c) STOP and report blocker. Option (b) validity depends on Phase 1 finding. Do NOT create scoring bands from memory — see prompt line 349: 'Do not create a new scoring model from memory.'

CONFIRMED from P1-1 §6.1: pkg_kb52c_tsh_high_primary_hypothyroid_pattern and pkg_kb52c_tsh_low_thyrotoxic_pattern are NOT runtime-loaded per package_estate_KB-S49_v1.yaml. The most clinically complete TSH clinical-pattern packages are inactive. Domain implementation using only kb47 FT3/FT4 signals without TSH as a governed signal would produce a domain card without scored TSH — a significant clinical gap. Prompt lines 381-384 require Cursor to determine whether an empty or TSH-lacking allowlist makes the thyroid domain misleading. If it does, STOP and report blocker.

CONFIRMED from P1-1 §6.1: kb59 antibody packages (TPO-Ab, TgAb) are inactive — not runtime-loaded per package_estate_KB-S49_v1.yaml. tpo_ab and tgab entries exist in biomarkers.yaml but have no runtime signal packages. Do NOT include kb59 antibody signals in any domain signal allowlist. Prompt candidate biomarker scope (lines 253-259) includes 'thyroid antibodies only if present and governed' — they are not runtime-governed.

Prompt lines 370-384 mandate signal-id allowlist routing only — not broad system-field matching. Allowlist must contain only signals with clear runtime authority. Given kb52c TSH inactive and kb59 inactive, candidate allowlist for safe runtime signals is limited to: signal_free_t3_high, signal_free_t4_high, signal_free_t4_low (all runtime_active_canonical per P1-1 §6.3). FT3 low inclusion requires register drift resolution in Phase 1. If register drift is unresolved, FT3 low must NOT be included in the allowlist — the fail-closed gate applies.

Prompt lines 287-322 prohibit diagnostic wording for hypothyroidism, hyperthyroidism, thyrotoxicosis, T3-toxicosis, thyroiditis, Graves' disease, Hashimoto's disease, pituitary disease, non-thyroidal illness, low T3 syndrome. biomarkers.yaml free_t3 key_risks_when_high includes 'T3_toxicosis'; key_risks_when_low includes 'non_thyroidal_illness' — these are SSOT metadata, not output copy. Domain narrative copy must use preferred framing (prompt lines 306-311): 'thyroid-axis markers', 'thyroid hormone signalling context', 'markers that can relate to energy regulation', 'a pattern that may be worth discussing with a clinician'. Do not lift key_risks language directly into output copy.

changes.finding: Prompt lines 56-88 mandate: Layer C, Gemini and frontend must not perform thyroid/energy regulation reasoning. Clinical safety rules apply (non-diagnostic, lab-range grounded, traceable, non-dangerous). No frontend medical inference, no Gemini, no fallback parser logic (lines 76-79, 421-437). These are hard constraints regardless of Phase 1 outcome — they apply to documentation files as well as runtime files.

Prompt explicitly prohibits: Knowledge Bus source package modification, Pass 3 artefact modification, AUTHORITY_MAP update, final strategy baseline update, Gemini, frontend inference, fallback parser logic, activating blocked/context-dependent signals, hardcoded clinical thresholds, global/default reference ranges, diagnostic thyroid disease claims. No duplicate authority sources detected.

All shell commands in prompt use PowerShell-compatible syntax: git switch, git pull, git status --short, git switch -c, git diff --stat, git diff --name-only, git status --short, python -m pytest --collect-only. No bash-only constructs.

overall_verdict: HARDENED — All five front-matter fields confirmed (automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:2-6). All four P1-3-completion prerequisite files confirmed on main (P1-1, P1-2, P1-3 docs and BUILD_DELIVERABLE_REGISTER). All 15+ authority paths verified to exist. Key source content read and cross-referenced: scoring_policy.yaml hormonal system EMPTY/INERT (lines 31-34: system_weight 0.0, biomarkers []); biomarkers.yaml thyroid entries confirmed (tsh:713, free_t4:678, free_t3:1158, tgab:698, tpo_ab:1177); P1-1 §6 thyroid section fully read — signal status, register conflicts, implementation-readiness judgement cross-referenced; P1-3 implementation pattern read and confirmed. THREE CRITICAL RISKS elevated for Phase 1 gate: (1) FT3 low register drift irreconcilably split across 3 governance registers per P1-1 §6.3:240 — probable Phase 1 STOP condition 1 trigger; (2) hormonal scoring system inert — unlike P1-2/P1-3, no existing scoring rail can be reused; (3) kb52c TSH packages inactive — domain without TSH scoring may be misleading, triggering prompt lines 381-384 STOP check. Signal routing must use explicit signal_id allowlist; candidate safe signals limited to signal_free_t3_high, signal_free_t4_high, signal_free_t4_low pending Phase 1 reconciliation; FT3 low excluded until register conflict resolved. Safety language STOP gates confirmed — SSOT key_risks metadata must NOT appear verbatim in output copy. Regression targets named: test_p1_2, test_p1_3, test_domain_score_assembler_v1, test_domain_ux1c_governed_subsystem_evidence, test_batch2_thyroid_tsh_gating, test_runtime_context_evaluation. Cursor may proceed under TWO_PHASE_START_FINISH governance: Phase 1 authority gate is the decisive step before any runtime modification.
